title: EANPV: Company XYZ is lost between three projects: A, B, and...
summary: Compare mutually exclusive projects with different lifespans using NPV or equivalent annual NPV analysis.
prompt: Company XYZ is lost between three projects: A, B, and C. A requires an initial investment of $10,000 and generates $7,000 for the coming two years. B requires $15,000 and generates $6,500 for the coming three years. C requires an initial investment of $20,000 and generates $6,000 for the coming 6 years. A, B, and C are mutually exclusive. Which project should XYZ take if its discount rate is 14%?
correct_option: Project A should be selected EANPV A : 926.74 EANPV B : 39.024 EANPV C : 856.85
answer_text: Project A should be selected EANPV A : 926.74 EANPV B : 39.024 EANPV C : 856.85
solution: To determine which project Company XYZ should take, we need to calculate the Net Present Value (NPV) of each project at the given discount rate of 14%. The project with the highest NPV should be selected. ### Project A: - Initial Investment: $10,000 - Cash Flows: $7,000 for 2 years - Discount Rate: 14% \[ \text{NPV}_A = -10,000 + \frac{7,000}{(1+0.14)^1} + \frac{7,000}{(1+0.14)^2} \] \[ \text{NPV}_A = -10,000 + \frac{7,000}{1.14} + \frac{7,000}{1.2996} \] \[ \text{NPV}_A = -10,000 + 6,140.35 + 5,384.62 \] \[ \text{NPV}_A = 1,524.97 \] ### Project B: - Initial Investment: $15,000 - Cash Flows: $6,500 for 3 years - Discount Rate: 14% \[ \text{NPV}_B = -15,000 + \frac{6,500}{(1+0.14)^1} + \frac{6,500}{(1+0.14)^2} + \frac{6,500}{(1+0.14)^3} \] \[ \text{NPV}_B = -15,000 + \frac{6,500}{1.14} + \frac{6,500}{1.2996} + \frac{6,500}{1.4815} \] \[ \text{NPV}_B = -15,000 + 5,701.75 + 5,001.54 + 4,387.79 \] \[ \text{NPV}_B = 91.08 \] ### Project C: - Initial Investment: $20,000 - Cash Flows: $6,000 for 6 years - Discount Rate: 14% \[ \text{NPV}_C = -20,000 + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^1} + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^2} + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^3} + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^4} + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^5} + \frac{6,000}{(1+0.14)^6} \] \[ \text{NPV}_C = -20,000 + \frac{6,000}{1.14} + \frac{6,000}{1.2996} + \frac{6,000}{1.4815} + \frac{6,000}{1.6889} + \frac{6,000}{1.9230} + \frac{6,000}{2.1871} \] \[ \text{NPV}_C = -20,000 + 5,263.16 + 4,615.38 + 4,050.42 + 3,553.89 + 3,120.61 + 2,745.27 \] \[ \text{NPV}_C = 352.73 \] ### Conclusion: - NPV of Project A: $1,524.97 - NPV of Project B: $91.08 - NPV of Project C: $352.73 Since Project A has the highest NPV, Company XYZ should select Project A.
